title: Michelin: Buyback Support And Structural Tailwinds Reinforce The Long-Term Case
article_text: Michelin remains rated Buy, supported by strong fundamentals, capital allocation flexibility, and upside as industry cycles normalize. Key drivers include an aging vehicle parc and a new upside: heavier electrified powertrains will likely accelerate tyre wear and innovation across divisions, supporting volume and margin growth. Share buybacks (~€400M) provided technical support; future capital allocation signals, including potential M&A, are expected at the FY25 update in February.

title: Michelin: It's Almost Time To Go Long Again
article_text: Michelin (ML) faces a weaker 2025, with revenues and margins down, but remains profitable and free cash flow positive. MGDDF's updated guidance lowers 2025 free cash flow to €1.5–1.8B, below prior targets, reflecting North American softness. Despite missing 2026 segment income targets, Michelin maintains its €5.5B cumulative FCF goal for 2024–2026.

title: Michelin: Not Priced For A Volume Recovery, Buy Confirmed
article_text: Michelin remains a buy, supported by pricing power and strong US production, despite recent volume declines. Q1 results showed robust price-mix effects and financial resilience, with guidance reaffirmed. Innovation continues across segments, and with the upside from cyclical recovery, we see Michelin as a compelling investment.

title: Michelin: Looking At The Upside For 2025-2028
article_text: Michelin offers a compelling 15%+ annualized upside at current €32.60/share, supported by strong fundamentals, A-ratings, and a 4%+ dividend yield. The company's diversified product mix, exposure to premium and specialty segments, and ongoing operational efficiencies offset industry cyclicality. Valuation is attractive at 11x P/E, below historical averages, with conservative fair value set at €45/share for 2027E, providing downside protection.

title: Tire Producers: Healthy Dividends With Defensive History
article_text: The tire manufacturing industry deserves the attention of defense minded investors looking for safe yield. Tire manufacturers' revenue and margins have been broadly stable through recent economic upsets; a tire's limited service life is independent of economic cycles. title: Michelin: Latest Acquisitions Will Likely Drive Competitive Advantage
article_text: Michelin is advancing with a clear M&A strategy to diversify sales and offset raw material inflationary pressure, focusing on digital transformation. The company's recent acquisitions include RoadBotics, a start-up specializing in road infrastructure image analysis, and sophisticated simulation software for tire development. Michelin's latest deal worth €700 million with FCG is expected to increase the High-Tech Materials turnover business by around 20% and position the company as a key player in polymer solutions.
